L1.area: Database driver layer and query construction logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied parameters used to construct a database sequence name are concatenated directly into a SQL query string without proper escaping, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL statements.

L3.area: PostgreSQL database driver and its sequence query construction logic
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied parameters used to construct a PostgreSQL sequence name are concatenated directly into a SQL query string without proper escaping. When the application connects to a PostgreSQL database and retrieves the last inserted identifier, an attacker can inject arbitrary SQL statements through the crafted sequence name components.

ground_truth.reason: The pg_insert_id() method concatenates user-supplied $tablename and $fieldname parameters directly into a SQL query without escaping, allowing arbitrary SQL injection when connecting to a PostgreSQL database.
